refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 s).

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model
Batch update mechanism to replace the entire worksite model at once
Event-driven update trigger to initiate model updates upon relevant changes
Scheduled periodic update trigger to refresh the model at fixed intervals
Parallel processing of update data to reduce latency
Prioritization of critical update elements to meet timeliness constraints
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model

coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates the update process to only changed elements, reducing coupling with unrelated model parts and supporting independence in update handling.

assumptions_risks: Incremental updates assume that changes can be detected and isolated efficiently.
Risk that complex dependencies in the worksite model may cause hidden coupling, impacting update latency.
Timeliness target (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) may be challenging under high update load or complex change sets.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the update triggering mechanism (event-driven vs scheduled) as a separate DP to further decouple update initiation from update application.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing and updating the worksite model, including applying incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and status data relevant to the worksite model and sends updated model state.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and update instructions that may trigger incremental updates to the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems to synchronize worksite model changes incrementally.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution's responsibility; minimal diffusion as updates are localized here.
Vehicle module primarily handles vehicle-specific functions; incremental update logic would be diffused and scattered if placed here.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring; incremental update mechanism would be peripheral and cause high diffusion.
Safety Services is focused on safety alerts and monitoring; incremental update mechanism is unrelated and would cause significant diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism logically belongs within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for managing the worksite model. This placement ensures high cohesion and minimal diffusion, as the mechanism is central to the module's function and does not require spreading across unrelated modules.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
If the worksite model updates require real-time safety checks, some coordination with Safety Services might be needed, risking slight diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to the DP is recommended; however, ensure interface definitions between NGHE Solution and other modules are well specified to maintain modularity and minimize diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness. Incremental updates logically reduce latency and processing overhead, supporting the acceptance criterion of update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
If worksite model updates require real-time safety checks, coordination with Safety Services might cause slight diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). Other modules show low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate. Interface boundaries are clearly defined, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring update latency metrics and ensuring the NGHE Solution module correctly processes incremental updates via its defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with the module scope explicitly including the incremental update mechanism and related interfaces.

recommended_revisions: No revision to the DP is recommended; however, ensure interface definitions between NGHE Solution and other modules (Telemetry, Network Link, Safety Services) are well specified to maintain modularity and minimize diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear justification and no recommended revisions to the DP or FR. The incremental update mechanism DP directly addresses the FR with acceptable latency, and the IM allocation to the NGHE Solution module is well contained with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, the current design includes careful interface definitions to minimize diffusion risk. Therefore, no further decomposition into sub-FRs is necessary at this stage.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the timeliness and safety requirements of the worksite model update FR.
Interface definitions between NGHE Solution and other modules will be maintained to minimize diffusion risk.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry, Network Link, and Safety Services interfaces may require ongoing monitoring to avoid tight coupling and diffusion.
Real-time safety checks coordination might introduce slight diffusion that needs management.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on update latency metrics to verify p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Interface specification documents confirming clear boundaries and modularity between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Verification reports demonstrating correct processing of incremental updates within NGHE Solution module.
